msgs.text:
I treated the prior turns as a proposal log, not as proof. The prompt fixes the tame-format submission: first generators sigma,tau, implicit relation τσ=τ2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and profinite exponents represented modulo M=85667662080.The only candidate not already refuted in the prior log is still the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9–39.

The surviving candidate is:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with π=π2∈Z and η=2π on odd tame inertia,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,where π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η≡50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of η is immaterial here, since that exponent is applied only to the tame generator τ, whose finite images have odd order.The main audit result this turn is that Turn 39’s final “ESTABLISHED” formulas are still inverted. The conclusions can be salvaged, but the displayed fractions must be corrected.For the sign-cover calculation, let V=kerκ, let t be the deck involution, and work on a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block τ↦α=1. ForNa (u)=(uτa)π,the Fox coefficient is the completed geometric sumDa=j=0∑π−1(tαa)j.

The correct identity isDa=1−tαa 1−(tαa)π=1−tαa1−t.

This is because π≡1(mod2) and π≡0(mode), so (tαa)π=t. This direction is essential: 1−tαa is a 2-adic unit for αa=1, while 1−t is radical. Hence Da is radical, and the coefficient fromx−1(xτ−η)π is −1+D−η, a unit. Thus the intended conclusion survives: nontrivial odd tame-character blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots. But the correct formula isDa=1−tαa1−t,not its reciprocal.The trivial tame-inertia block has a similar direction issue. With T the σ-cycle operator and s≡π(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T−s)X+(1+T)Y=0.

If f=2a m with m odd, then s≡1(mod2a) and s≡0(modm). For even f, s is odd, so 1+T∣1+T−s. Therefore the valid elimination is

Y=P(T)X+Yˉ,P(T)=1+T1+T−s,up to the harmless convention T↔T−1. With h(T)=1+P(T)+P(T−1),one gets, for a nontrivial f-th root ζ,(1+ζ)h(ζ)=ζ−s+ζs+1=ζ−s(1+ζ2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), gcd(f,2s+1)=1. So h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and the trivial block remains nonsingular. The correct formula is therefore

P(T)=1+T1+T−s,not its reciprocal.With those corrections, the prior abelianization consequences still stand:Ue,fab≅Z2ef+1⊕C2,and for the sign kernel,(kerκ)ab≅Z22ef+1⊕C4.The new structural refinement is about Turn 38–39’s mixed (e,f)=(3,2) class. In that quotient, writeΔ3,2≃S3=⟨τ,σ∣τ3=σ2=1, στσ−1=τ2⟩,and setAi=Xτi,Bi=Xτiσ,i∈Z/3.

Turn 38 computed that the candidate gives α(b)b−1≡Z0 (modU3),Zd=i∈Z/3∑[Ai,Bi+d].

Turn 39 correctly sharpened this to the relation-preserving tangent representativeδ0 (b)=Z0,δ0(Ai)=[Bi,S],δ0(Bi)=[Ai,S],δ0 (S)=0.

However, the label Z0 is not gauge-invariant until the tame Frobenius lift is fixed. If one replaces the quotient Frobenius generator by another valid tame liftσ′=τk σ,k∈Z/3,while keeping τ fixed, thenBi′=Xτiσ′=Xτi+kσ=Bi+k.

Consequently,Zd′=i∑[Ai,Bi+d′]=i∑[Ai,Bi+d+k]=Zd+k.

So the statement “the candidate selects Z0 ” is meaningful only after choosing the standard arithmetic Frobenius lift, for example the lift fixing the chosen tame uniformizer in L=Q2(ζ3,32). Without that gauge choice, the invariant target is the C3 -orbit{Z0,Z1,Z2},or, more cohomologically, that the class has a nonzero augmentation-zero mixed component rather than lying purely in the unramified-looking lineN=Z0+Z1+Z2.This changes the next arithmetic target. It is too rigid to demand “arithmetic gives exactly Z0 ” before fixing the same Frobenius lift. The correct immediate target is:

For the standard tame Frobenius fixing32, does arithmetic give δ0? Equivalently, without fixing that gauge, does arithmetic give one of the three relation-preserving classes δd attached to Zd? This does not prove the presentation, and it does not refute the live candidate. It narrows the comparison problem: the mixed (3,2) class is a genuine filtered-action datum, but its exact d=0,1,2 label is partly a Frobenius-lift convention.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I corrected two inverted formulas from the latest prior turn:Da=1−tαa1−t,P(T)=1+T1+T−s.

With these corrections, the prior conclusions Ue,fab≅Z2ef+1⊕C2 and (kerκ)ab≅Z22ef+1⊕C4 survive. I also identified a gauge issue in the mixed (3,2) target: replacing σ by τk σ cycles Zd↦Zd+k, so “Z0 ” is meaningful only after fixing the arithmetic Frobenius lift.

GAP: The candidate is still not a proved presentation of GQ2. The arithmetic side must be shown to realize the same relation-preserving mixed class δd, with the same Frobenius-lift convention, and then the comparison must be extended to the full filtered even-Frobenius action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: Fix the standard S3 -extension L=Q2(ζ3,32) and the Frobenius lift that fixes32. Compute the arithmetic action on GL (2)/GL(2)3 or GL (2)/GL(2)5, with the precise target that it matches the relation-preserving class δ0; if a different Frobenius lift is used, compare only up to the Zd -cycle.[BREAKTHROUGH]
